metadata.target: AI协作项目全生命周期框架 v1.5

review_context.framework_summary: AI协作项目全生命周期框架 is a meta-level methodology specification describing how to run complete projects with AI collaboration. It covers a lifecycle from Spec (L0) through Closure (L5) plus cross-layer concerns.
v1_5_additions: §6.2 模式8: Pipeline DAG (声明式管道执行) — from GitNexus runner.ts/types.ts
§6.2 模式9: Structured Multi-Role Review (结构化多角色审查) — from GitNexus PR Swarm Review
§9.2 升级: 多轮多后端独立审查编排 — from GitNexus R1→R2→R3 review实战
§9.6: 证据分类与声称校准 (S/E/I/J/Sp五级) — from R2 review trigger + R3 validation

five_issues_found: Mode 8/9 not integrated into decision tree/templates — HIGH
additional_design_fixes: Mode 8 trigger revised from 'stage count >= 3' to 'branching dependencies or hidden-coupling risk'
Mode 9 Solo mode independence caveat added
Multi-round review blind-first assessment rule added
Decision tree and upgrade triggers updated for Mode 8/9

review_history.focus: factual accuracy, analytical rigor, independence, methodology quality, completeness
process soundness, domain depth, utility/actionability, epistemological hygiene
decision relevance, competitive grounding, narrative framing, meta-analysis
new content quality, integration/coherence, methodological soundness, actionability

encoding_note: CRITICAL: All Chinese content is UTF-8 encoded. If you see garbled characters, explicitly set encoding to UTF-8.

1_fix_verification.description: Verify that the 5 R4 issues were genuinely fixed
checklist: Is the JSON truly synced to v1.5 (version field, evidence_classification section, provenance_notes)?
Are metadata contradictions resolved (header, status line, footer all say v1.5)?
Is GitNexus provenance properly annotated with evidence labels [I]/[S] and source caveats?
Is the evidence taxonomy self-applied to v1.5 changelog entries?
Are Mode 8/9 integrated into the decision tree (§6.3) and upgrade triggers (§6.4)?
1_fix_verification.also_check: Did any of the fixes introduce new inconsistencies (regression)?

2_cross_backend_perspective.description: Use your Qwen/Alibaba backend to see what ChatGPT and Kimi reviewers could not
questions: What do you notice that a ChatGPT-family reviewer might be blind to?
Are there assumptions reflecting Western/American AI development norms?

The framework was written in Chinese by a Chinese-speaking author. Both you and Kimi are Chinese-backend models. Does this shared cultural context reveal anything the ChatGPT reviewers missed — or does it create its own blind spots?
Does the framework's China-centric perspective (Zhihu/CSDN sources, Chinese AI ecosystem references) hold up under your scrutiny?

3_practical_usability.description: After 5 versions and all fixes, is the framework actually usable by a new practitioner?
questions: If handed this framework to run a project, what would your first 30 minutes look like? Where would you get stuck?
Which sections would you actually reference during a real project? Which would you skip?
Has the framework become a methodology encyclopedia that needs a 'quick start' extraction?
Rate the onboarding experience on a scale of 1-10.

4_methodology_maturity.description: The framework labels itself '草案，待试跑验证' — evaluate whether this is still honest after 5 versions
questions: Is 'draft' still an honest label, or has it become a shield against accountability?
What specific conditions would justify moving from draft to v1.0 stable?
The framework draws from 6+ completed projects. Is this sufficient empirical basis?
After 5 rounds of self-referential review, is dogfooding generating diminishing returns?
What is the single most important thing needed before 'stable'?

file_1.path: AI协作项目全生命周期框架_v1.5_独立审查报告_R5.md
sections: 1. Reviewer Identity — declare model (Qwen3.7-Max), confirm zero-involvement, note relationship to R1-R4
2. Fix Verification — per-issue verification with evidence
3. Cross-Backend Perspective — what you see that prior reviewers didn't
4. Practical Usability — onboarding score, first-30-minutes walkthrough, reference-worthy vs. skippable sections
5. Methodology Maturity — is draft still honest? conditions for stable?
6. Overall Assessment — independent grade, comparison to R4's C+
7. Metadata — model, date, backend, relationship to prior rounds

file_2.path: AI协作项目全生命周期框架_v1.5_独立审查报告_R5.json

critical_reminders: You are the FIFTH reviewer. Don't repeat R1-R4 — read them, acknowledge them, then add what they missed.
Your Qwen backend is the key value of this round. Notice things ChatGPT and Kimi reviewers couldn't.
The framework is a draft — don't penalize honesty. DO evaluate whether 'draft' is still fair after 5 versions.
The authors just fixed 5 issues per R4. Acknowledge fixes, but don't let them excuse remaining problems.
Read EVERY file completely before writing anything.
